bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by asking for my top value on every issue. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue, so I should counter firmly rather than reward an extreme opening.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has moved only slightly, from all valueF to valueE on issueA while holding the rest at valueF, so their offers remain very low utility to me and do not yet justify major concessions. I will concede first on issueD because it has the lowest importance weight for me.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for me while making a small concession to avoid stalling. The opponent has moved from all valueF to improving only issueA, which raises my utility somewhat, but they have not yet signaled meaningful flexibility on other issues; I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD for now.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has conceded only on issueA while holding issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF, so I will keep my high-weight issues A/B/C at valueA and make a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD, while avoiding repeating my previous exact bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, so I should not concede on the higher-weight issues A, B, or C yet. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA, but they continue to hold issueD at valueF, so I will concede fully on the lowest-weight issueD while maintaining my core preferences.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still very early, while making a real concession beyond my last offer by moving issueC from valueA to valueB and keeping issueD at valueF. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on those higher-weight issues while conceding on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.856 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to concede on the most important issues yet. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA and then issueB, but they are still holding issueC and issueD at valueF; I will keep issueA and issueB at my top values while making a further concession on the lower-weight issueC and already conceding issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.824, since it is early at t=0.14 and the opponent has been steadily conceding toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB. Their latest valid offer is much better for me than their opening but still leaves issueC and issueD at my worst values, so I will concede further on lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.79, conceding further on the lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me up to issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA, but they still keep issueC and issueD at valueF; since it is still early at t=0.16, I can ask for a modest improvement on issueC rather than immediately matching their latest bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, since it is still early and the opponent has been steadily conceding upward toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB. Their latest offers show they may be firm on issueC and issueD at valueF, but issueC is more important to me than issueD, so I will keep issueD conceded while asking for a better issueC value and avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84, staying above the opponent’s latest offer to me while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid. At t=0.20 there is still ample time, and the opponent has clearly conceded toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB while only beginning to move on issueC, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues and make only limited concessions on lower-weight ones.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, keeping my high-weight issues A and B at valueA while making concessions on the lower-weight issueD. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA and issueB and recently moved issueC from valueF to valueE, so I will ask for a further move on issueC to valueD while matching their apparent preference on issueD. Time is still early enough not to accept their latest 0.79-equivalent offer outright.

At t=0.24 it is still early enough to avoid accepting the opponent’s current A/A/E/F offer, which gives me about 0.79 utility, but they have clearly conceded on issueA and issueB and only recently moved issueC to valueE. I am holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B, while making a modest tradeoff by accepting their issueC movement to valueE and asking for a small improvement on the low-weight issueD instead of repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, keeping strong positions on the high-weight issues A and B while making concessions on lower-weight issueD. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA and issueB and is now offering A/A/E/F, so I will counter by improving issueC to valueD for me while leaving issueD at their repeatedly signaled valueF. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I do not need to fall all the way to their latest offer yet.

I am targeting a utility around 0.805, which is still strong for me while making a modest concession at this early-mid stage. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA and issueB to my top values but is holding firm around issueC=valueE and issueD=valueF, so I will accept their movement on issueC while asking for a small improvement on my lowest-weight issueD rather than repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding substantially on issueC and mostly on low-weight issueD. The opponent has steadily improved their offers up to issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA, and their latest offer on issueC reached valueD while they remain firm on issueD/valueF; since time is still only 0.30, I will make a modest counter rather than fully matching their last bid.

I am targeting a utility around the mid-0.8s: still favorable because time is only 0.32, but responsive to the opponent’s steady concessions toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB. The opponent has now signaled willingness to accept issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, and issueC=valueD while holding issueD=valueF, so I keep their issueD position and ask for a modest improvement on issueC rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.839 by holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding on lower-weight issueC/issueD. The opponent has steadily improved their offers for my utility and most recently offered issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueF, so I am matching their concession on issueC while asking for a small improvement on low-weight issueD. At t=0.34 there is time left, so I do not need to drop all the way to their current bid yet.

I targeted a utility around 0.85 by holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while using lower-weight issueD as the main concession. The opponent has clearly conceded to valueA on issueA and issueB and is currently offering issueC at valueD while holding issueD at valueF, so at t=0.36 I will test a slightly better issueC outcome for me while matching their strong preference signal on issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.839, conceding mainly on the lower-weight issueC while keeping the high-weight issueA and issueB at my best values. At t=0.38 there is moderate time pressure but not enough to immediately accept the opponent’s repeated issueD=valueF position, especially since their recent offers have stabilized at issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA with issueC/valueD. There are no opponent messages, and the bidding suggests they may accept issueC=valueD while still pushing hard on issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.856, keeping my high-weight issues A and B at valueA while conceding the low-weight issueD fully. The opponent has consistently offered issueA and issueB at valueA and is now holding around issueC: valueD, issueD: valueF, so at t=0.40 I will test whether improving issueC to valueC while giving them issueD is still acceptable.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.824, which is still strong for me while improving the chance of agreement as we approach the midgame. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA, and recently stabilized at issueC=valueD, issueD=valueF, so I am conceding on the low-weight issueD rather than risking another cycle. There are no opponent messages to weigh, so I am relying only on the offer pattern.
